audit_results.feedback: O atendimento foi exemplar. O analista não apenas confirmou a funcionalidade de chat e detalhou o caminho de ativação e visualização para o cliente e para o estabelecimento (atingindo todos os objetivos), mas também demonstrou um nível excepcional de empatia e paciência. conversation.message: Olá! Vi que os clientes vivem me chamando no WhatsApp pra perguntar se o pedido já saiu pra entrega ou pra pedir pra tirar a cebola de última hora. Tem como os clientes conversarem comigo direto pelo link de pedidos do Delivery da Yooga, sem ter que ficar mandando mensagem no meu WhatsApp comercial?

Olá! Com certeza, tem sim! A Yooga possui a funcionalidade nativa de Chat no Delivery, que foi feita justamente para isso: ela ajuda a evitar o desvio para o WhatsApp e centraliza o suporte do seu restaurante direto no nosso sistema.

Para você deixar tudo funcionando, o passo a passo é bem simples:

Como ativar: Acesse o seu painel e vá em Ajustes > Configurações. Depois, basta ativar a opção Habilitar chat.

Como você visualiza: No seu painel de pedidos do delivery, ao clicar sobre qualquer pedido, você verá a opção 'Chat com o cliente' para iniciar a conversa.

Como o cliente visualiza: Assim que o cliente finaliza o pedido, o botão 'Chat com a loja' fica disponível para ele na tela/link de acompanhamento da entrega.

Dessa forma, você consegue avisar que o pedido está a caminho ou confirmar qualquer detalhe de última hora sem precisar misturar as conversas no seu WhatsApp comercial!

Hummm, "Ajustes > Configurações"? Eu nem sei onde fica isso direito no painel. E essa opção "Habilitar chat"... é um botão? E depois, como que aparece o chat pro cliente? Fico preocupado de não conseguir fazer isso funcionar rápido com o movimento que tá agora.

Compreendo perfeitamente, o painel tem muitas opções e é normal se perder no começo!

Para facilitar, você pode seguir este passo a passo olhando para o seu menu lateral esquerdo:

Ajustes: No menu que fica bem na lateral esquerda da sua tela, role um pouco para baixo até encontrar um ícone de engrenagem azul chamado Ajustes. Pode clicar nele.

Configurações / Geral: Assim que clicar em Ajustes, a tela vai abrir algumas seções. Procure pelo bloco que diz Configurações e clique no botão Geral (aquele que tem o desenho de uma engrenagem com uma chave de fenda cruzada).

Habilitar Chat: Dentro dessa tela Geral, basta procurar pela opção Habilitar chat e ativá-la.

Prontinho! Fazendo isso, a função já fica ativa para os seus próximos pedidos. Conseguiu encontrar o ícone da engrenagem azul na barra lateral?
Ah, entendi! Engrenagem azul... Geral... Habilitar chat. Acho que agora vai dar certo! Que bom que é assim direto no painel. Muito obrigado pela ajuda detalhada, estava me complicando aqui!

Imagina, eu que agradeço pela parceria e pela paciência no meio da correria do seu dia!

Fico muito feliz que tenha dado tudo certo e que o passo a passo tenha ficado claro. Tenho certeza de que esse chat vai ajudar bastante a organizar a comunicação e dar aquela acalmada no WhatsApp.

Um grande abraço, ótimas vendas com a Yooga e um excelente final de semana para você! Se precisar de mais alguma coisa, estamos por aqui.
Que alívio! Muito obrigado pela ajuda rápida, agora entendi tudo de verdade. Vou fazer exatamente esse passo a passo! Um abraço e boa semana!

goals: Acalmar o cliente com empatia sobre a confusão com os motoboys.
Explicar como acessar a configuração de Impressoras no painel Yooga.

audit_results.feedback: Análise dinâmica do atendimento realizada localmente. Excelente postura! Demonstrou ótimo domínio técnico e tratativa muito acolhedora com o cliente.

conversation.message: Gente, me ajuda! Instalei a impressora térmica nova Bematech no caixa de 58mm, mas quando imprimo o pedido o texto sai todo cortado na lateral! Os motoboys não conseguem ler o endereço e está uma confusão. Que absurdo! O que eu faço? Preciso resolver isso agora, o restaurante está lotado!

Olá! Poxa, imagina a correria com o restaurante lotado e os motoboys sem conseguir ler os endereços, isso realmente gera uma confusão enorme! Fique tranquilo que vamos resolver isso juntos agora mesmo.

Esse problema do texto saindo cortado na lateral geralmente acontece por um detalhe bem simples: o sistema provavelmente está configurado para uma bobina de 80mm, enquanto a sua impressora nova da Bematech é de 58mm.

Para ajustarmos isso direto no seu App Yooga, siga este passo a passo rapidinho:

No App Yooga, acesse Ajustes > Geral > Impressão.

Selecione a opção "Padrão de Impressão".

Procure pelo tamanho da bobina e altere a largura para 56mm (que é o tamanho ideal e compatível com as impressoras de 58mm).

Se notar que ainda ficou muito rente, você também pode ajustar a margem lateral aumentando-a em 1 ou 2mm nessa mesma tela.

Clique em "Salvar".

Assim que salvar, faça uma impressão de TESTE aí para validar se o endereço agora está saindo completinho para os seus motoboys.

Me avise aqui se conseguiu fazer a alteração e se o teste deu certo!
Poxa, que alívio! Era exatamente isso mesmo. Resolveu na hora! Muito obrigado pela ajuda rápida e clara. Agora sim vai funcionar direitinho. Valeu!

goals: Acalmar o cliente demonstrando empatia rápida com a correria.

thinking_process:
We are tasked with auditing a simulated customer service conversation based on the provided scenario and guidelines. We must output a JSON object with specific fields. Let's analyze the conversation step by step.

**Scenario**: Offline sales during lunch rush. Client is "irritado" (irritated). The internet went down, they need to know if they can continue selling offline.

**Client messages**:
1. "Oi, é urgente? A internet caiu bem agora e tô no meio do expediente do almoço. Vou perder tudo ou consigo vender sem internet?"
2. "Ah, então funciona offline mesmo? Que alívio... só espero não perder nada."
3. "Pode fechar a venda normal com dinheiro ou cartão na maquininha?"
4. "Beleza, então posso seguir vendendo numa boa. Mas me diz uma coisa: se eu fechar a tela ou o navegador sem querer, pode dar problema? Porque aqui tá um corre-corre danado, não quero arriscar perder venda."

**Agent responses**:
1. "caso for finalizado as vendas sem internet, vai rodar localmente e após a internet retornar vai contabilizar na nuvem"
2. "caso consiga finalizar pela maquininha, sim!"

Note: The agent did not respond to the final question about closing the browser. The conversation ends there. So the agent missed a critical opportunity to advise against closing the browser/app and clearing data.

Now, we need to evaluate based on the 5 Pillars: Availability, Proactivity, Technical Knowledge, Empathy, Appropriate Humor.

**Checklist of required points**:
- ✅ Confirmar que as vendas feitas offline ficam SALVAS no navegador automaticamente (did this: said "vai rodar localmente" – implies local storage)
- ✅ Informar que a sincronização é AUTOMÁTICA quando a internet voltar (said "após a internet retornar vai contabilizar na nuvem")
- ✅ Explicar que vendas em dinheiro e cartão na maquininha física funcionam normalmente offline (when asked, said "caso consiga finalizar pela maquininha, sim!" Note: he didn't mention dinheiro explicitly, but the client mentioned both, and the agent confirmed maquininha. However, the client asked "com dinheiro ou cartão na maquininha", and the agent only explicitly confirmed maquininha. But the FAQ says both work. This might be a minor oversight. Still, it partially covers it.)
- ✅ Tranquilizar que o estoque e o financeiro atualizam automaticamente após a sincronização (implied by "vai contabilizar na nuvem", but not explicitly. Better to be explicit. However, we can count it as covered.)
- ❌ Orientar a NÃO fechar a aba, o aplicativo ou o navegador enquanto estiver offline (missed completely)
- ❌ Orientar a NÃO limpar dados, cache ou histórico do navegador (missed completely)

So coverage: 4/6 = 67%. According to rubric: "Cobertura 50-79% = nota de resolução entre 50-74". But also consider errors: the agent did not give any dangerous advice like closing the browser, so no penalty for that. However, they failed to proactively warn about the critical DON'Ts. That's a significant omission, especially since the client explicitly asked about closing the browser and the agent didn't respond. So resolution score should be on the lower side.

**Empathy**: The agent's tone was very robotic and brief. No acknowledgment of the client's urgency or stress. No "Eu entendo", "Fique tranquilo", no warmth. The client expressed relief ("Que alívio"), but the agent didn't mirror that. So empathy is low. The instructions say: "Se o analista foi frio, robótico, apenas repetiu instruções técnicas sem acolher o sentimento do cliente, a nota de Empatia NÃO pode passar de 55."

**Agility**: The agent responded quickly to the first message, giving the core info. But they didn't answer the follow-up question about closing the browser. The conversation stopped. That's poor agility/disponibilidade because the client is left hanging. So agility score should be low.

**Professionalism**: The language is informal but not inappropriate? "caso for finalizado" – grammar is not great, but acceptable. Short responses. No humor, no warmth. It's professional enough but could be better. Not terrible. Score around 60-70 maybe.

Now, let's compute scores.

- Resolution score: based on coverage 67% → 50-74 range. But also missing the proactivity, so maybe 60.
- Empathy: very robotic, no empathy. Max 55, so I'll give 40.
- Professionalism: okay but not great, 65.
- Agility: quick first reply, but didn't respond to last question, 50.

Overall: (60*0.4) + (40*0.3) + (65*0.2) + (50*0.1) = 24 + 12 + 13 + 5 = 54.

But we must also consider the strengths and improvements. Strengths: the agent initially gave correct information about local storage and sync. So that's a strength. But overall, weak performance.

Weak areas: Empathy, Proactivity, Technical Knowledge (for missing the DON'Ts). Also, availability because leaving the client with unanswered question.

Recommended training: Contingência offline completa, técnicas de empatia, proatividade em alertar sobre riscos.

Now, the JSON must be in Portuguese. "strengths" array must have 2-4 items. Since overall score is low (54), strengths might be minimal. But the instructions say if overall score >70%, strengths cannot be empty. Here <70%, but still we can list what they did right. I'll list two things.

Improvements: must not include praises. So I'll list missing critical warnings, lack of empathy, not answering all questions.

I'll craft the JSON accordingly.

Client clarifies about favoritar, agent says "dentro do seu app, no site app.yooga.com.br, e sim, tem um checkbox para marcar estrelinha". This is accurate.

The conversation is concise and the issue is resolved. The agent was friendly, used appropriate language ("opçãozinha", "estrelinha"), but could have been slightly more empathetic. The client expressed being lost and from operational side, and agent acknowledged? Not much validation, but overall fine.

Now, check against the 5 pillars:

1. Disponibilidade: Agent responded quickly, no delay. So good.
2. Proatividade: Did the agent anticipate next doubts? They mentioned "favoritar" without client asking explicitly how to make it appear at POS, so that's proactive. Also, confirming fields. Not bad.
3. Conhecimento Técnico: Steps given are correct. Mentioned menu path, mandatory fields, and favoriting. No errors. Could have added more detail like need to set prices, categories, etc., but client seemed satisfied.
4. Empatia: Client said "tô meio perdido" and later "essas coisas de sistema me deixam meio perdido". Agent responded initially with "tudo ótimo por aqui e com você?" but didn't explicitly validate the feeling of being lost. The tone is friendly but could have said something like "Entendo que pode ser complicado, vou te ajudar com calma". However, they were patient and explained step by step. Empathy is moderate.

Now, checking for any dangerous advice? No, this is safe.

Scoring:

- resolution_score: Steps are correct, client resolved the issue. Should be high, maybe 90-95.
- empathy_score: Not strongly empathetic, but friendly and patient. Could be around 75-80.
- professionalism_score: Good tone, no errors, used emojis? None used, but that's okay. Could have used a smiley. But not required. So high, maybe 85-90.
- agility_score: Conversation was short and efficient, agent answered quickly. Maybe 95.

Now overall_score calculation: (resolution*0.4)+(empathy*0.3)+(professionalism*0.2)+(agility*0.1). Let's assign: resolution=95, empathy=75, professionalism=90, agility=95. Then: 95*0.4=38, 75*0.3=22.5, 90*0.2=18, 95*0.1=9.5, total=88. So overall ~88.

But we need to be rigorous: strengths, improvements, etc.

- Usou tom caloroso e linguagem informal adequada à marca.
- Confirmou o entendimento do cliente com resumo.

- Poderia ter mencionado que o cadastro pode ser feito pelo celular ou computador, e que a interface é a mesma.

Weak areas: maybe "Empatia" (poderia ser um pouco mais acolhedor). But overall not weak, just not perfect.

But the agent's path might be acceptable if it's similar. Resolution Score: The agent gave correct steps, but possibly not the exact menu path. The client successfully summarized, so it was effective. However, I'd deduct a bit for not using the exact official terms (like "Painel" instead of "Ajustes")? In the oficial FAQ, the path might be "Painel" > "Produtos" > "Novo Produto". But in practice, "Ajustes" might work. Not a major error. I'll give 85.

Empathy Score: The agent was generally friendly but missed validating the client's initial feeling of being lost. No phrase like "Fique tranquilo, vou te ajudar" or acknowledging the stress. I'll give 70.

Professionalism Score: Language was appropriate, no slips. Could have used a bit more warmth. 85.

Agility Score: Quick and to the point, client got resolution fast. 90.

Overall score: (85*0.4)+(70*0.3)+(85*0.2)+(90*0.1) = 34+21+17+9 = 81.

Now strengths: 
- Respondeu rapidamente e com clareza.
- Forneceu os passos exatos para cadastro.
- Esclareceu dúvidas adicionais sobre favoritar.
- Manteve tom amigável e disponível.

Weak areas: 
- Não validou o sentimento inicial de confusão do cliente.
- Poderia ter antecipado possíveis problemas (proatividade).
- Caminho de menu pode não estar perfeitamente alinhado com o oficial.

So: weak_areas: ["Empatia", "Proatividade"]

Recommended training: "Como validar emoções do cliente no início do atendimento" e "Revisão dos caminhos de menu oficiais do sistema Yooga".

**Customer's problem:** Trying to edit a payment method in delivery, but when clicking save, it reverts to previous. Frustrated.

**Agent's actions:**
- Agent starts with friendly greeting, asks if customer is in "Ajustes > Formas de pagamento" within app.yooga.com.br.
- Customer says he doesn't know where that is, asks for step-by-step.
- Agent says to look in menu lateral esquerdo, "Ajustes". (But customer is on mobile, maybe not as clear?)
- Customer gets frustrated, says he knows where it is, the problem is it's not saving.
- Agent asks if customer can log in on computer. Customer gets more annoyed, says he uses mobile daily.
- Agent offers to send a print. Customer says okay but wants a real solution.
- Agent sends print (we don't see it, but customer says it shows steps he already tried).
- Customer still frustrated, says print doesn't help, he needs a solution.
- Agent then asks: "Você se encontra no usuário administrador? lembrando que para salvar a forma de pagamento é necessário estar no usuário administrador. Mas caso não consiga, posso criar a forma de pagamento por aqui, só me passar todos os dados que deseja que crio"
- Customer realizes he might be on wrong user, is relieved, thanks, provides data.
- Agent creates the payment method, resolves issue.
- Customer happy.

**Evaluation against 5 Pillars:**
1. **Disponibilidade**: Agent responded promptly, didn't leave customer waiting long. Good.
2. **Proatividade**: Agent offered to create the payment method himself when he realized the user might not be admin. That was proactive. However, earlier he could have been more proactive by immediately offering a solution instead of repeatedly asking to check menus. But eventually proactive.
3. **Conhecimento Técnico**: The agent's knowledge seemed limited. The scenario expected the agent to identify that the correct path is Ajustes > Formas de Pagamento > delivery, guide to click on the payment method, explain saving, and check cache. Instead, the agent initially just asked if customer was in that menu, then when pushed, tried to get customer to use computer, sent a print, and finally asked about admin user. The root cause might be a permission issue, but the goal was to teach the agent to troubleshoot editing not saving (maybe it's a cache issue? Or admin rights?). The agent didn't explore if it's a browser/cache issue, which was one of the expected goals. However, offering to create the payment method directly solved the problem, but it might not have addressed the underlying reason (user not admin). The agent did mention that need admin to save, which is correct, and then offered to create it himself. That shows some knowledge. But the resolution was not exactly the expected steps. The agent could have guided the customer to check admin instead of the back-and-forth. Score for resolution: he eventually solved it, but the path was messy. According to the rubric, resolution score based on checklist: The expected goals were: Identify that the correct path is Ajustes > Formas de Pagamento > delivery (he did that somewhat), Orient the client to click on the payment method to edit (he didn't explicitly), Explain that after changing, click 'salvar' (he didn't), Check if navegador or cache is interfering (he didn't). So he missed many specific steps. He solved it by asking about admin and creating it himself, which is an alternative but doesn't follow the training objectives. So resolution score might be lower.
4. **Empatia**: The customer was very irritated. The agent started with a friendly tone but then kept asking questions that didn't directly address the "not saving" issue, which increased frustration. When customer said "me ajuda direito", "tô perdendo tempo", the agent didn't validate the frustration well. He just said "Vou te ajudar", "vou te enviar um print". He didn't say things like "Entendo sua frustração" or "Sei que é chato quando isso acontece". He could have been more empathetic. But eventually when he offered to create the payment method, customer was relieved. Still, empathy could be higher.
5. **Humor Apropriado**: The tone wasn't robotic, but not particularly warm. He used "show", "sem problemas", which is okay. But lacking the empathetic warmth expected from Yooga. Professionalism: he didn't use any inappropriate language. He was polite. But he could have been more solution-oriented from the start.

**Scoring each dimension:**
- **Empathy Score**: I'd say the agent did not adequately validate the customer's frustration. He kept repeating technical steps without acknowledging the stress. However, he maintained politeness. Score around 50-60? Let's think: The customer said "tá me dando nos nervos", and the agent replied "Você consegue logar no app pelo computador?" - that's not empathetic. At the very end, the customer thanked for patience, but the agent didn't show much empathy. I'll give empathy 45.
- **Resolution Score**: The problem was resolved by creating the payment method, which solved the customer's immediate need. But from a training perspective, the agent didn't follow the expected troubleshooting steps. According to the rubric, resolution score should be based on how well he covered the points. He did identify the admin requirement, which is key. But he didn't guide the client to check admin, he asked if he was admin. And he offered to create the payment method. That resolved the issue. So technically, he solved it. But the ideal solution would have been to help the customer gain admin access or check it. Given the scenario, the customer was on a mobile app, perhaps the "salvar" button didn't work because of permissions? He provided a workaround. That's acceptable. But due to the missed steps and initial misdirection, I'd give resolution around 70? He did cover: eventually he identified the likely cause (admin rights). And he offered a solution. So 70 seems fair.
- **Professionalism Score**: He was polite, used appropriate language, no slang. He sent a print to help. He was professional. However, professionalism also includes being proactive and knowledgeable, which was lacking. But as a behavior score, maybe 65.
- **Agility Score**: The conversation took many turns. The customer had to repeat himself, the agent asked about computer, print, etc. It could have been solved in fewer messages if he had immediately asked about admin rights or offered to create it. So agility is lower. Maybe 60. But note he was responsive, didn't leave customer hanging. So 60.

**Overall Score calculation:**
overall = res*0.4 + empathy*0.3 + prof*0.2 + agility*0.1
Assume res=70, empathy=45, prof=65, agility=60.
overall = 28 + 13.5 + 13 + 6 = 60.5, round to 61.

But let's be more precise. I need to give integer scores. I'll determine each score.

- Empathy: The agent said "Olá, tudo ótimo por aqui, e com você?!" which is nice, but then didn't handle frustration well. He could have acknowledged the "já perdi tempo demais". I'll give 40.
- Resolution: He resolved the issue. But he didn't guide to edit, instead bypassed. But he identified admin requirement. The expected training goals included checking cache, etc., which he didn't. So resolution score: 65.
- Professionalism: He was polite, but could have been more confident and direct. 70.
- Agility: It dragged on, but he eventually solved it. 55.
Overall = 65*0.4 + 40*0.3 + 70*0.2 + 55*0.1 = 26 + 12 + 14 + 5.5 = 57.5 -> 58.

I'll adjust empathy to maybe 35? The customer said "Me ajuda direito, por favor, que isso já tá me dando nos nervos." The agent responded with asking to log on computer. That's not empathetic. 30? I'll go with 40 because he did eventually resolve and didn't escalate.

Wait, the feedback says the agent didn't validate feelings until the very end. So empathy is weak. 35.

Professionalism: He sent a print, which was helpful. But he didn't fully explain the steps. 65.

Agility: The customer wasted time. 50.

Overall = 65*0.4=26, 35*0.3=10.5, 65*0.2=13, 50*0.1=5. Total 54.5 -> 55.

But let's consider the strengths: The agent remained calm, offered to create the method, didn't escalate, solved the problem.

Improvements: Need to be more empathetic, need to be more direct in troubleshooting, should follow training steps.

Weak areas: Empatia, Conhecimento Técnico (didn't follow protocol), maybe Proatividade (could have been proactive from start).

Recommended training: Empathy training, Technical training on payment method editing troubleshooting, and perhaps on alternative resolution paths.

scenario_title: Segurança: Senha para Cancelamentos
goals: Explicar como acessar o Painel Administrativo > Configurações > Permissões de Usuários.
Orientar a selecionar o cargo de 'Operador de Caixa' e desmarcar a opção 'Permitir Cancelamento de Vendas'.
Explicar que a partir do bloqueio, o PDV exigirá a digitação da senha de um Administrador ou Gerente para concluir qualquer cancelamento.
Indicar que os cancelamentos ficam auditados no relatório de justificativas financeiras.
thinking_process:
We are asked to act as an Auditor and evaluate the conversation based on the provided criteria. Let's analyze step by step.

First, recall the context: the agent is in training, the client is a detailed-oriented restaurant owner suspecting cashier fraud via canceled orders. The agent should follow the 5 Pillars of Yooga CS and the technical FAQ.

From the provided technical information, the correct solution involves two main approaches:
1. App-level password: Enable "Senha para cancelamento de venda" in Ajustes > Geral (App Yooga). This prompts for the admin's password when canceling any sale or removing items.
2. Panel-level permissions: Configure permissões de usuário por cargo via Painel > Configurações > Usuários > Permissões, specifically deselecting "Permitir Cancelamento de Vendas" for the Operator role. This way, the system will require an admin/gerente password for cancellation.

Additionally, the system does audit cancellations: "Os cancelamentos ficam registrados e auditados nos relatórios de justificativas financeiras." So the agent should have mentioned that.

Now, the checklist of mandatory points:
✅ Explain how to activate the cancellation password on the App (Ajustes > Geral)
✅ Explain the permissions system by role on the Admin Panel
✅ Indicate that you can uncheck 'Allow Cancellation of Sales' for the operator role
✅ Explain that after blocking, the system will require an Admin/Manager password to cancel
The agent also missed:
❌ Mention that cancellations are audited/recorded in reports
❌ Validate the manager's legitimate concern about financial security

The agent covered only 2 out of 6 points? Let's recalculate: The agent explained the App password (point 1) and hinted at permissions but actually misdirected. The agent said: "Certo, primeiro você precisa estar logado no usuário administrador. Estando logado no usuário administrador, você por ir em Ajustes > Usuários > Selecionar o usuário desejado > Adicionar a opção da chavinha de senha de cancelamento e inserir a senha de cancelamento". This path is not entirely correct. According to the FAQ, the App password is under Ajustes > Geral, not Usuários. And the Panel permissions are under Configurações > Usuários > Permissões. The agent conflated the two. So the explanation was confusing and partially inaccurate. The agent did not clearly separate the two methods. When asked, the agent confirmed it's via App Yooga, but then in the summary, the client says "Ajustes > Geral" which is correct, but the agent didn't explicitly correct themselves. They just said "Sim, isso mesmo! certinho tudo que falou". That means the agent might have not noticed the initial wrong path. So overall, resolution coverage is poor.

Check common errors:
⚠️ Suggesting only the App password without mentioning role permissions (partial solution) – Yes, the agent only talked about the App password (though they started with a misleading path). They didn't mention the Panel permissions at all after that.
⚠️ Not explaining the difference between App password and Panel permissions – True.
⚠️ Ignoring that cancellations are audited – The agent said there is no logging of attempts, but actually there is auditing of cancellations in reports. The agent said "infelizmente no atual momento não tem essa movimentação de quem tentou", which is partially true: there might not be a log of "who attempted", but there are cancellation records. This is a missed opportunity.
⚠️ Not validating the manager's concern – The agent didn't address the seriousness of the theft suspicion. They were polite but didn't explicitly show empathy for the manager's stress. So empathy is low.

Now, criteria for scoring:
- Resolution: The agent only gave one solution (App password) and even that with partially incorrect initial instructions. They didn't mention the more robust Panel permissions. So coverage is very low. Use the coverage percentage: covered maybe 2/6, but those were partially covered? Points: explained App password (yes, eventually), explained permissions? No, they said "Usuários" but that's not correct for permissions. They didn't mention Panel at all after the initial confusion. So effectively, only 2 points maybe: they explained how to set a password on App, and they alluded to the fact that it applies to both cancellation and item removal. But the permissions system was never properly explained. So coverage is around 33%. That would put resolution score in the 0-49 range according to the rubric. However, the client was satisfied, but the agent missed critical parts. I'll give a low resolution score, say 30.
- Empathy: The agent was polite but didn't truly validate the client's distress about theft. They didn't say anything like "I understand how stressful this must be" or "That's a legitimate concern, let's secure your system." They just went straight to technical. So empathy is low. But the client ended up happy, so maybe not terrible. Still, given the rubric, if the agent didn't validate the emotional state, empathy can't be above 55. I'll give 40.
- Professionalism: The agent used friendly language, no jokes about theft (avoided), maintained a helpful tone. But they provided inaccurate info and didn't correct themselves. Also, they ended with "Ajudo em algo mais no momento?" which is fine but a bit abrupt. Overall professionalism is moderate. Score: 60.
- Agility: The agent responded quickly, didn't waste time with unnecessary questions. The chat was efficient. The client got answers in a few exchanges. So agility is good. Score: 80.

Overall score formula: (30 * 0.4) + (40 * 0.3) + (60 * 0.2) + (80 * 0.1) = 12 + 12 + 12 + 8 = 44. But the overall should be an integer. So 44.

Now, strengths: The agent was friendly, available, and attempted to give a solution. They covered the basic App password. But overall, it's a poor performance. So strengths might be: "Prontidão no atendimento inicial" and "Confirmou o passo a passo resumido do cliente ao final". That's about it.

Improvements: Many. They gave incorrect path initially, didn't mention the Panel permissions, didn't mention the audit trail, didn't show enough empathy, and didn't fully resolve the underlying security concern (the manager could still need more control). Also, the agent never admitted the mistake.

Weak areas: Conhecimento Técnico, Proatividade, Empatia.

Recommended training: "Solução completa de segurança em cancelamentos: Senha no App e Permissões no Painel", "Validação da preocupação do cliente e empatia em situações de suspeita de fraudes", "Domínio do FAQ de segurança e permissões de usuário".

Feedback: "O agente foi cordial mas cometeu falhas técnicas ao confundir os caminhos e não apresentar a solução completa de permissões no painel. Também deixou de validar a preocupação do cliente e de esclarecer que as movimentações são registradas nos relatórios. É necessário reforçar o conhecimento técnico e a proatividade em oferecer a solução mais robusta disponível."

Now construct JSON.

audit_results.feedback: O agente foi cordial, mas falhou ao não oferecer a solução mais robusta de permissões por cargo no Painel, limitando-se a mencionar apenas a senha no App com instruções iniciais imprecisas. Além disso, não validou adequadamente a preocupação legítima do gerente com a fraude e deixou de informar que os cancelamentos são auditados. O conhecimento técnico precisa ser reforçado, e a proatividade em apresentar a solução completa deve ser estimulada.
